title: President von der Leyen in Gdańsk for the Ukraine Recovery Conference
content:
Tomorrow, President Ursula von der Leyen will participate in the Ukraine Recovery Conference, in Gdańsk, Poland.
Co-hosted by Poland and Ukraine, the conference scheduled for 25 and 26 June marks the fifth edition of this annual high-level event dedicated to mobilising international support for...

title: Commission simplifies and makes energy and tyre labelling rules more accessible to European consumers
content: European consumers, suppliers and retailers of home appliances, electronic devices and tyres are set to benefit from simplified rules on labelling proposed by the European Commission today.
snippet: European consumers, suppliers and retailers of home appliances, electronic devices and tyres are set to benefit from simplified rules on labelling proposed by the European Commission today.

title: Commission proposes new measures to better tackle cross-border crime and terrorism
content: The European Commission has proposed measures to strengthen the EU’s response to an evolving criminal landscape that is becoming more sophisticated, international and digital.
snippet: The European Commission has proposed measures to strengthen the EU’s response to an evolving criminal landscape that is becoming more sophisticated, international and digital.

title: VET reforms across Europe: Cedefop's updated Timeline tool shows 700 measures in motion
content: New data from Cedefop's updated online tool Timeline of VET policies show that vocational education and training reforms across EU-27, Norway and Iceland are moving forward, with countries shifting from planning to active implementation.

title: Shaping the European Housing Alliance: turning principles into action
content: The European Housing Alliance held its inaugural meeting in Brussels on 19 June, marking a critical step in implementing one of the priorities of the European Affordable Housing Plan adopted by the European Commission in December 2025.

title: EU-backed innovative approaches to combating the trafficking of goods
content: The workshop 'Innovative Approaches to Combating the Trafficking of Goods' was hosted by the Commission in Brussels, bringing together over 70 participants to address innovative European solutions in the fight against the illicit trafficking of goods, focusing on wildlife and intangible assets.

title: Apply before 16 August: Do you have direct experience with social, affordable and sustainable housing financing?
content: The Commission is looking for stakeholders with direct experience with financing of social, affordable and sustainable housing. A new working group will bring expertise together and share best practices.

title: Two-thirds of gas appliances fail EU checks
content: EU-funded testing of a selection of gas appliances like barbecues, gas stoves and camping cookers found that 73 % failed to meet EU requirements for performance and product documentation.
snippet: EU-funded testing of a selection of gas appliances like barbecues, gas stoves and camping cookers found that 73 % failed to meet EU requirements for performance and product documentation.

title: Statement by President von der Leyen following the EU-Moldova Summit
content: Dear Maia, Madam President, Dear António, Only last week, we opened the first negotiation cluster with Moldova. It is a historic milestone on your European journey, it is opening the door to the accession process to the European Union, and it is a recognition of Moldova's determination...

title: Suspension of auctioning of Member States Recovery and Resilience Facility allowances
content: The auction calendar has been adjusted following the suspension of the auctioning of the first leg of emission allowances allocated to the Recovery and Resilience Facility (RRF).
snippet: The auction calendar has been adjusted following the suspension of the auctioning of the first leg of emission allowances allocated to the Recovery and Resilience Facility (RRF).

title: Commissioner Šuica meets Israeli authorities and Palestinian Authority
content:
Today and tomorrow, Commissioner for the Mediterranean, Dubravka Šuica, will meet with Israeli authorities and the Palestinian Authority to reiterate the EU's support for peace and stability in the Middle East.
In Jerusalem, she will meet with Israeli President Isaac Herzog and Foreign Minister...

title: Commission publishes first annual social report on fisheries, aquaculture and fish processing
content: The European Commission has published the first annual social report on fisheries, aquaculture and processing sectors, providing an overview of the social aspects of all three sectors. The report, prepared by the Scientific, Technical and Economic Committee for Fisheries (STECF),

title: EU-Moldova Summit in Brussels marks a new milestone on path to EU membership
content: Today, the second EU-Moldova Summit marked another step forward in EU–Republic of Moldova relations and underscored the country's remarkable progress on its EU path.
snippet: Today, the second EU-Moldova Summit marked another step forward in EU–Republic of Moldova relations and underscored the country's remarkable progress on its EU path.

title: Report reveals the skills, sectors and trends driving a sustainable ocean future
content: The blue economy is experiencing significant growth driven by sustainability objectives, technological innovation, climate action and emerging ocean industries.
snippet: The blue economy is experiencing significant growth driven by sustainability objectives, technological innovation, climate action and emerging ocean industries.

title: President von der Leyen co-hosts EU-Moldova Summit in Brussels
content: On Monday, President of the European Commission Ursula von der Leyen and President of the European Council António Costa will host the second EU-Moldova Summit.
snippet: On Monday, President of the European Commission Ursula von der Leyen and President of the European Council António Costa will host the second EU-Moldova Summit.

title: EU delivers €34 million in immediate support to Armenia as announced by President von der Leyen
content: Today, the European Commission has disbursed €34 million to Armenia to help mitigate the impact of Russia's trade restrictions on the country's private sector. Just two weeks after President von der Leyen's call with Prime Minister Pashinyan...

title: Support for EU accession remains high across enlargement partners, latest surveys show
content: Today, the European Commission published the results of the latest perception surveys on enlargement among citizens in the Western Balkans, Eastern Neighbourhood and Türkiye. The findings show that support for EU accession remains strong across the enlargement partners...

title: Joint statement by the European Commission and the High Representative ahead of World Refugee Day
content: On World Refugee Day, we will recognise the strength and resilience of the more than 117 million people displaced globally.
snippet: On World Refugee Day, we will recognise the strength and resilience of the more than 117 million people displaced globally.

title: Commission strengthens Europol to step up the fight against cross-border crime and terrorism
content: The Commission proposed new measures to strengthen the EU’s response to an evolving criminal landscape. New proposal cover the full chain of EU support to Member States, from prevention, detection and investigation to judicial cooperation and effective prosecution.

title: Commission imposes anti-dumping duties on imports of BDO from three countries
content: Today, the European Commission published a Regulation imposing definitive anti-dumping duties on imports of 1,4-Butanediol (‘BDO’) originating in the People’s Republic of China, Saudi Arabia and the United States of America.

title: EU & China step up dialogue on biodiversity protection and finance, plastic pollution and global environmental governance
content: The discussions in Brussels allowed both sides to deepen environmental engagement and coordinate positions ahead of crucial global summits.
snippet: The discussions in Brussels allowed both sides to deepen environmental engagement and coordinate positions ahead of crucial global summits.

title: European Commission proposes landmark tax simplification package to streamline compliance and boost competitiveness
content: The European Commission adopted an ambitious tax simplification package that includes two legislative proposals aimed at simplifying EU tax rules, reducing compliance burdens for businesses, and strengthening the competitiveness of the Internal Market.

title: EQAVET Peer Review on stakeholder engagement in VET in Portugal
content: The peer review provided an opportunity to reflect on how stakeholders, particularly employers, can contribute more effectively to quality assurance (QA) in vocational education and training (VET).
snippet: The peer review provided an opportunity to reflect on how stakeholders, particularly employers, can contribute more effectively to quality assurance (QA) in vocational education and training (VET).

title: Solving the affordability crisis for young Europeans
content: If rising costs are affecting you as a young person in Europe, then find out what solutions were proposed at this year’s Youth Policy Dialogue in Brussels.
snippet: If rising costs are affecting you as a young person in Europe, then find out what solutions were proposed at this year’s Youth Policy Dialogue in Brussels.

title: EU at the London Climate Action Week: Joining forces to expand electrification and reduce methane emissions
content: At the 8th edition of the London Climate Action Week, with the backing of the European Commission, important international milestones were reached on expanding electrification and reducing methane emissions.
snippet: At the 8th edition of the London Climate Action Week, with the backing of the European Commission, important international milestones were reached on expanding electrification and reducing methane

title: Study examines how blue bioeconomy could diversify Northwest fisheries
content: A study by Bord Iascaigh Mhara (BIM) Ireland’s Seafood Development Agency, and Donegal County Council (DCC), examines how the blue bioeconomy could help diversify the fisheries sector in Northwest Ireland, particularly the pelagic fishing industry.
snippet: A study by Bord Iascaigh Mhara (BIM) Ireland’s Seafood Development Agency, and Donegal County Council (DCC), examines how the blue bioeconomy could help diversify the fisheries sector in Northwest

title: Simplifying EU rules to support competitiveness
content: By simplifying EU rules on energy products and taxation, the latest omnibus proposals will help support both businesses and people.
snippet: By simplifying EU rules on energy products and taxation, the latest omnibus proposals will help support both businesses and people.

title: Joint call on current and prospective donors to contribute to the Ukraine Energy Support Fund
content: Over the past four years, G7+ countries have channelled €1.95 billion through the Ukraine Energy Support Fund, managed by the Energy Community Secretariat, making a substantial contribution to Ukraine’s energy resilience. title: Bonn climate conference keeps momentum towards COP31 with technical negotiations advancing and new action agenda announcements
content: The Bonn UN Climate Change Conference concluded with progress on key technical issues, helping to advance implementation of the Paris Agreement and prepare the ground for COP31 in Türkiye. New initiatives and discussions focused on turning climate commitments into practical action.
snippet: The Bonn UN Climate Change Conference concluded with progress on key technical issues, helping to advance implementation of the Paris Agreement and prepare the ground for COP31 in Türkiye. New

title: Using art to promote environmental action in Águeda's schools
content: Águeda, European Green Leaf 2026, brought circular economy learning into schools as Year 5 students transformed recyclable materials into wildlife-inspired urban artworks.
snippet: Águeda, European Green Leaf 2026, brought circular economy learning into schools as Year 5 students transformed recyclable materials into wildlife-inspired urban artworks.

title: European Union signs aviation agreement with Kazakhstan
content: The EU and the Republic of Kazakhstan have signed a Horizontal Aviation Agreement.
snippet: The EU and the Republic of Kazakhstan have signed a Horizontal Aviation Agreement.

title: EU launches Connectivity Agenda Platform to strengthen links between Europe and Central Asia through the Black Sea region and the South Caucasus
content: Today, the European Commission launched the Connectivity Agenda Platform and concluded agreements with international financial institutions expected to mobilise up to €2 billion for strategic connectivity investments in the Black Sea region and the South Caucasus.
snippet: Today, the European Commission launched the Connectivity Agenda Platform and concluded agreements with international financial institutions expected to mobilise up to €2 billion for strategic

title: Third EU-Serbia Horizon Europe Joint Research and Innovation Committee meeting
content: The meeting discussed the Serbian participation in Horizon Europe and latest policy developments.
snippet: The meeting discussed the Serbian participation in Horizon Europe and latest policy developments.

title: Finalists announced for the 2026 EU Organic Awards
content: The 2026 EU (European Union) Organic Awards finalists have been announced, with 21 finalists from 13 EU countries. The winners will be announced at an official ceremony in Brussels on 23 September.
snippet: The 2026 EU (European Union) Organic Awards finalists have been announced, with 21 finalists from 13 EU countries. The winners will be announced at an official ceremony in Brussels on 23 September.

title: New Commission guidance will make it easier to transfer ownership of SMEs
content: Today, the European Commission published a new Recommendation on business transfers. Facilitating the transfer of ownership of small and medium enterprises (SME) responds to a pressing demographic challenge.

title: EU High Risk Security Network organised a specialised training for law enforcement authorities
content: The EU High Risk Security Network organised the third edition of the EU-funded counter unmanned aircraft systems, commonly known as ‘drones’, training cycle for law enforcement, hosted by the Portuguese Guarda Nacional Republicana Training Centre, located in Figueira da Foz, Portugal.

title: The European Commission welcomes NEAFC measures on mackerel and deplores Russia’s further quota increase in 2026
content: The mackerel stock in the North-East Atlantic is currently in a critical conservation status after years of overfishing, particularly by the Russian Federation. In 2026, Russia unilaterally raised its quota to 22.5% of the Total Allowable Catch (TAC) - equivalent to 67,548 tonnes

title: Guimarães grows greener: a community in action
content: Organised as part of the 'Guimarães 2026 – European Green Capital' programme, the International Conference on Sustainability in Sport, Eco Parliament and Green Week Guimarães events brought communities together to promote climate action, education, active citizenship and a more sustainable future.
